content: Good morning everyone. I am happy to start this project.
Today I studied Prisma and it is easier than I expected.
Next.js API routes are very useful for simple student projects.
I like building simple social media apps using JavaScript.
This is my first post in the app.
Working on comments and likes now.
Frontend and backend together make the project stronger.
SQLite is simple and good for class projects.
Statistics page is important for phase 2.
I finished follow and unfollow feature.
Just joined the platform. Excited to connect with everyone!
Learning React has been a great experience so far.
Database design is the backbone of any good application.
Finished my first API endpoint today. Feeling proud!
Teamwork makes the dream work.
CSS is harder than I thought but I am getting better.
Reading documentation is a skill every developer needs.
I love how simple SQLite is for small projects.
Debugging is like solving a puzzle. I enjoy it!
Our graduation project is coming together nicely.
Morning coffee and coding. Best combination ever.
Just fixed a bug that took me two hours to find.
Tailwind CSS makes styling so much faster.
I prefer working on backend over frontend.
Version control with Git saved my project today.
Prisma queries are clean and easy to read.
Submitted my assignment on time. What a relief!
Started learning TypeScript. It adds great safety.
The statistics page looks great with real data.
I enjoy reading other people's code to learn new tricks.
Building this social media app taught me a lot.
API testing with Postman is very helpful.
Clean code is better than clever code.
Planning before coding saves a lot of time.
Finally understand how relations work in databases.
Next.js makes full stack development so smooth.
Helped a friend fix her code today. Felt great!
Dark mode should be a default in every app.
Learned about indexes in databases. Very useful for speed.
Writing seed data is actually kind of fun.
Responsive design is a must for modern web apps.
I wish I started learning programming earlier.
Group projects teach you patience and communication.
Just discovered a new VS Code extension. So useful!
Error handling is something I need to improve on.
Presentation day is coming. Need to prepare well.
The follow feature was the hardest part for me.
Completed all the required features for phase two!
Looking forward to feedback from the instructor.
This project made me love web development even more.
